note: Log(avg fare) regression coefficients; MHHI delta and HHI on the 0-1 scale (10,000 points = 1.0). Market-level specifications chosen because this tool's data is market-level. Verified against the papers' tables 2026-07-05 (see docs/plans/02-hour-zero-verifications.md).

regimes.label: AST (2018) — common ownership raises fares
regimes.citation: Azar, Schmalz & Tecu (2018), 'Anticompetitive Effects of Common Ownership', J. Finance 73(4):1513-1565, Table 3 col. (6): market-level, full controls, 2001Q1-2014Q4.

regimes.label: DGS (2022) — the effect is statistically zero
regimes.citation: Dennis, Gerardi & Schenone (2022), 'Common Ownership Does Not Have Anticompetitive Effects in the Airline Industry', J. Finance 77(5):2765-2798, Table V Panel B col. (4): IV specification, market-level. Coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero.

context.replication: DGS first replicate AST's market-level estimate almost exactly (0.196 vs 0.202; DGS Table II Panel B) before their corrections — the disagreement is about assumptions, not data errors.
context.decomposition: DGS Tables III-IV: placebo ownership + true market shares still yields a significant positive coefficient (0.212), while true ownership + placebo market shares yields ~0 or negative (-0.144) — their case that the correlation lives in the market-share component.
context.refutation: AST, 'A Refutation of...' (SSRN 4158149, 2022); DGS, 'A Surrebuttal...' (SSRN 4307814, 2022). The fight remains open.
context.framework: O'Brien & Salop (2000), Antitrust Law Journal 67:559 — the MHHI framework.
